Assembly: The assembly ilAleLoef3.1 is based on 169x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned by synteny to GCA_963966035.1.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Vetigastropoda; Trochida; Trochoidea; Trochidae; Cantharidinae; Steromphala
Assembly: The assembly of Steromphala pennanti (xgStePenn6.1) is based on 71X of PacBio data and 162X of Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 67 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial), totaling 11.66 Mb (with the largest being 7.95 Mb). Additionally, 272 regions totaling 49.16 Mb (with the largest being 6.07 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 5 haplotypic regions and and 4 contaminant sequences were removed, totaling 4.29 Mb and 0.31 Mb, respectively (with the largest being 1.47 Mb and 0.11 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilGraOrob1.hap2.1 is based on 45x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were identified by copy number in the diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W (37.7 - 38.1, 44.6 - 45.2, 47.3 - 47.7 Mbp), chromosome 20 (11.1 - 11.8 Mbp) and chromosome 25 (8.3 - 9.0 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly icLacStri1.hap2.1 is based on 60x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome X identified by identification of ancestral X BUSCO genes. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 5 (18.0-106.0 Mbp) and chromosome X (57.9-63.4 Mbp). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (38.9 - 43.3 Mbp) are unknown.

Taxonomy_x: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Artiodactyla; Ruminantia; Pecora; Cervidae; Odocoileinae; Rangifer
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Artiodactyla; Ruminantia; Pecora; Cervidae; Odocoileinae; Rangifer
Assembly: This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly mRanTar2.1 of Rangifer tarandus tarandus, common name mountain reindeer, a subspecies of reindeer. The sample was provided by Anne Eline Streitlien and Atle Mysterud, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). mRanTar2.1 is based on 30x PacBio HiFi data and 88x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm along with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies (i.e., reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to homology to mRanTar1. Both sex chromosomes are included in hap1.

Assembly: The assembly idSceJere3.hap1.1 is based on 129x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome Y and X is unknown and is based mostly on HiC signal. The sex chromosomes were identified by read coverage. The naming of X and Y was based on heterochromatic region size and BUSCO (diptera_odb12) gene count.

Assembly: The assembly ilSpiOrbi1.hap2.1 is based on 108x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Description: Rangifer tarandus platyrhynchus genome assembly, chromosome: 32
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Artiodactyla; Ruminantia; Pecora; Cervidae; Odocoileinae; Rangifer
Assembly: On Mar 19, 2025 this sequence version replaced OX596116.1.; This project provides the alternate haplotype genome assembly of Rangifer tarandus platyrhynchus, common name Svalbard reindeer, a subspecies of reindeer, based on a sample provided by Vebjorn Veiberg. The assembly is provided by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The assembly mRanTar1.2 is based on 32x PacBio HiFi data and generated by the EBP-Nor project. The assembly was filtered for adapters with HiFiAdapterFilt., assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to get two sets of reads to scaffold the assemblies (reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, before putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. No mitochondrial genomes were found in the data, so NC_007703.1 was added to hap1 for completeness. Both X and Y chromosomes are found in hap1. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly xbBatHeck2.1 is based on 30x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly ilParFuli1.1 is based on 54x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosomes Z and W were assigned based on read coverage statistics.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Chordata; Craniata; Vertebrata; Euteleostomi; Mammalia; Eutheria; Laurasiatheria; Artiodactyla; Ruminantia; Pecora; Cervidae; Odocoileinae; Rangifer
Assembly: This project provides the genome assembly of Rangifer tarandus platyrhynchus, common name Svalbard reindeer, a subspecies of reindeer, based on a sample provided by Vebjorn Veiberg. The assembly is provided by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The assembly mRanTar1.h1.1 is based on 32x PacBio HiFi data, 56x Arima Hi-C data and generated by the EBP-Nor project. The assembly was filtered for adapters with HiFiAdapterFilt., assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to get two sets of reads to scaffold the assemblies (reads used to scaffold hap1 did not contain k-mers unique to hap2). YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, before putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. No mitochondrial genomes were found in the data, so NC_007703.1 was added to hap1 for completeness. Both X and Y chromosomes are found in hap1. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Spiralia; Lophotrochozoa; Mollusca; Gastropoda; Vetigastropoda; Trochida; Trochoidea; Trochidae; Cantharidinae; Steromphala
Assembly: The assembly xgSteCine2.1 is based on 42x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Large inversions between sister chromatids can be seen on chromosome 5 29.7-60.7mb and chromosome 11 17.7-39.7mb. Possible inversions on chromosome 11 3.4-39.4 and 18-66mb.
